category_title: Elementary-aged children with mild/moderate autism and significant language delays
minimal_context: The student and a peer are playing with grocery store toys on the carpet in a sectioned-off hallway.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are going to play with the grocery store toys on the carpet for ten minutes. You and your partner can play together.

STUDENT: (Picks up a plastic apple) Red apple.

TUTOR: Your partner has the shopping basket.

STUDENT: (Looks at the basket) Basket.

TUTOR: What can you say to your partner?

STUDENT: (Reaches for the basket without asking) Put apple.

options.A: Help the student compliment their partner and rephrase any hurtful comments into something more friendly while they are playing with the grocery store toys.
options.B: Suggest roles and ways to talk (e.g., assigning a shopper and a cashier), and role-play and model ways to use the grocery store toys and communicate.
options.C: Work on the student's recognition of body language and facial cues with their partner while, at the same time, labeling feelings during the play session.
options.D: Have more new motivating grocery store toys handy to introduce to the student and their partner during the ten-minute play session.
